falsification_conditions.condition: The S&P 500 closes above 5,000 in 7 consecutive trading days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A sustained breakout above this key resistance level would contradict the 'HOLD' signal and indicate a bullish trend, invalidating the current verdict.

falsification_conditions.condition: The blowoff score exceeds 60 for 3 consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A blowoff score above 60 would indicate extreme volatility or momentum, directly contradicting the 'HOLD' signal and suggesting a market reversal or climax.

falsification_conditions.condition: The 14-day realized volatility of the Nasdaq drops below 15% and remains there for 7 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Prolonged ultra-low volatility would indicate market complacency or consolidation, falsifying the 'HOLD' signal which implies ongoing uncertainty or potential movement.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
